John Page Jul 31st, 2025 at 09:26
The way I explain it generally is that indexes are inherently sorted lists and
results are subsets sorted by the index key. If they are sorted the same way
then it is very easy to find the intersection of them by walking each from to
back. If they are sorted in different ways then you need to take each item in A
and find it in B so ON log N). Importantly though this assumes that you have B
in memory basically which can be pretty impractical.
Chris Harris Jul 31st. 2025 at 09:48
Yeah see also SPM-3809.
And yeah it's not that the plans don't work, it's that it's impossible for them to
win the plan selection process given our current scoring/ranking algorithm.
There is a knob that basically forces such plans to win. Would be curious if you
flipped it on and checked for performance changes in your apps what you'd
actually see @alcaeus...
